entry.title: Re: Create custom tab in XD02/XD03 transaction
entry.content: As Sandra wrote, it is a search help. Please read about it. There is plenty information online. You have to put in some work, but this is the right way to achieve your requirement.

entry.title: Re: 1H2025 Release: What is the purpose of the new Standard Element of Company
entry.content:
Hello,
SAP has introduced this standard element in employee profile. Most of the companies created a custom string in employee profile to capture this. SAP has not provided much information but the plan might be using this in the latest people profile(profile preview, spotlight or full profile), as they are more focused in that area.

Br,
Namratha

entry.title: Re: "Hidden" Gems: 10 Underused Features in SAP SuccessFactors Core HR
entry.content:
Sven,

We would love to use the SuccessFactors mobile app for viewing payslips, but it doesn't work when you have payroll on ECC.  We have a hybrid environment with SuccessFactors EC replicating to ECC and have ECC payroll (not SF EC Payroll) running in SAP.  The mobile payslip app only works with SF EC payroll.  This is very disappointing and a sore spot for us.  The employees have to use their web browser to access SuccessFactors payslip URL links (payroll mashup).  Of course we will eventually have to migrate to another payroll system, like SF EC Payroll, but until then we have to deal with it.

SAP should make the mobile app work for all SAP customers and not just those on your latest payroll system.  SAP is leaving a lot of old, loyal SAP Payroll customers behind.

entry.title: Text is not visible in lower level of level based hierarchy with compounded key
entry.content:
I have an Analytics model over SAP SD data on ECC

I have created a Customer Sales dimension with level based hierarchy with 3 levels

Customer_Hier1

Customer_Hier2

Customer

Each has a text view connected to it

In the Analytic model both via preview or SAC I can see the hierarchy correct with test on the upper levels but no text on the lower level of Customer

When I display the object with flat hierarchy I do see the customer text

Thanks

Shlomi

entry.title: Re: CDS view entity : how to use domain value in WHERE cl...
entry.content: why you need to have it? can't  u type the hardcoded value?

entry.title: Re: Sustainability Control Tower: Update on SAP-provided Metrics on ESRS regulation
entry.content:
Dear @RenataKZ,

thanks a lot for your comment. You are correct. Those metrics you can see here in the list are all "planned". The blue ones have been additionally added to the "planned" list as part of the blog update on May 7th. As soon as some of these metrics get released, we will mark those as such. You can expect further updates on the planned metrics as well as released metrics in the upcoming week.

Thanks a lot for your patience and best regards,
Franziska

entry.title: BDCQ - Finance - Needs to be in sync with SAP Best Practice Scope Items List
entry.content:
@SAPSupport Hi,

It appears to me that BDCQ (Business Driven Configuration Questionnaire)  for Finance  not in sync with  SAP Best practice scope items. E.g. Scope Item 1QM described as 'Advanced Financial Operations' in SAP Best practice scope items whereas same scope item described as 'Credit Evaluation and Management' in BDCQ.

 

 

Thanks

KP

entry.title: Re: FinOps Bookmarks Repository
entry.content:
Hi, While importing the package into our SAC tenant, we encountered the following error. Do you have the latest version of the package? Our tenant version is 2025.8.5.

Importing may not work for BTPRC FinOps Dashboard_2024.08_v2.0.0.package as it was created in an older version of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Do you have package which matches our tenant?

Regards,

Ganesha

entry.title: Re: Can the LDAP system be used for user details but igno...
entry.content:
Hi

In order to use the LDAP data source you need to enable the connector for the PROV scenario and include in the action 004. That makes technically the connector visible for requests.

Some alternatives (might be more):

- if you don't care that the connector is there and appears in ARM you can disable the actual provisioning in the Provisioning settings in SPRO and configure the connector to "no provisioning" as part of "system specific" provisioning settings

- If you pick a user and then select "existing assignments" the system appears because you've run the repository sycnch for the LDAP connector and you have the entry in GRACUSERCONN table. This is technically not needed if you use Realtime search for your datasource. parameter 2050 = YES. In such scenario each time you include a user in an ARM requests the user details are retrieved in realtime from the LDAP connector and and entry in GRACUSERCONN is not technically needed. that means you won't see the connector as part of "existing assignments" functionality. Realtime sych might impact performance in GRC and your LDAP connector so you might want to analyze the implementation carefully.

. You might be able also to use some restriction at auth object level to avoid the connector to be shown for some users.

Hope it helps

Diego

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Bengaluru, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, June 20th, 2025 at Mindset Consulting, Bengaluru 🇮🇳 from 10:00am - 15:30pm IST. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

The instructors will be Developer Advocates @Rekha_DR , @Shilpa_Shankar @sheenamk and @eshrinivasan.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 40 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Рᴇферальный код MᴇXC: (124UTp)
entry.content:
Торгуйте Выгодно с MᴇXC: Получите до 8 000 USDT Бонусов в 2025 Году

Если вы хотите начать торговать криптовалютой с минимальными комиссиями и при этом получать щедрые бонусы — платформа MᴇXC станет отличным выбором. Это одна из крупнейших криптобирж в мире с миллионами пользователей и интуитивно понятным интерфейсом.

Просто зарегистрируйтесь с рᴇферальным кодом (124UTp) и получите скидку 20% на торговые комиссии навсегда, а также шанс заработать до 8 000 USDT в виде приветственных бонусов.

entry.title: Rule Context for Onboarding 2.0 is not set to No
entry.content:
Hi ,

We have a rule configured in EC which sets the email adress , email type and IsPrimary to visibility to None.

This Rule is impacting Onboarding too, hence I tried to go to Rule Context and set Onboarding = No. it Threw a warning that Rule Context is not supported of event type "OnInit".

We dont want this rule to be enabled in Onboarding but it should remain functioning in EC due to GDPR. How can we achieve this ?

Would appreciate your help.

Thanks and Regards,

Parul

entry.title: Рᴇферальний код MᴇXC: (124UTp)
entry.content:
Торгуйте з вигодою на MᴇXC: Як Отримати До 8 000 USDT Бонусів у 2025 Році

Якщо ви хочете розпочати торгівлю криптовалютою з мінімальними комісіями та отримати щедрі бонуси — платформа MᴇXC стане ідеальним варіантом. Це глобальна біржа з мільйонами користувачів і зручним інтерфейсом.

Зареєструйтесь із рᴇферальним кодом (124UTp) — і отримаєте постійну знижку 20% на торгові комісії, а також можливість заробити до 8 000 USDT у вигляді вітальних бонусів.

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hello @mlauber,

Once the report is scheduled as application job, is there any change that we can run the report in foreground by assigning transaction to report as our requirement is that business user should be able to run the report in foreground as well and it is scheduled as background job for creating deliveries for the sales order.

Please suggest.

entry.title: Re: Is 5.0 fp01 release?
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The FRUN 5.0 FP01 version is now available when calculating the FRUN update in the Maintenance Planner. Please see SAP Note 3603061 - SAP Focused Run 5.0 Feature Package 01 - Update Preparation and Postprocessing Documentation, and plan the update. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Código de rᴇferencia de MᴇXC: (124UTp)
entry.content:
Ahorra a lo Grande con MᴇXC: Cómo Obtener Hasta 8 000 USDT en Bonos en 2025

Si estás buscando comenzar a operar con criptomonedas ahorrando en comisiones y obteniendo recompensas atractivas, MᴇXC es la plataforma ideal. Con millones de usuarios en todo el mundo y una interfaz intuitiva, es perfecta tanto para principiantes como para traders avanzados.

Solo necesitas registrarte utilizando el código de rᴇferencia (124UTp) para recibir un descuento permanente del 20 % en comisiones y desbloquear hasta 8 000 USDT en bonos de bienvenida.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hey friends!

The first time I heard about SAP was back in 2008, when I was working as a financial auditor. I came across a job posting from Indra (a Spanish consulting firm) looking for recent graduates to train in a strange accounting program called ERP SAP.

Back then, I’ll never forget the words of a President from a well-known bank in Buenos Aires (where I lived at the time).

He said:

“Give it a shot — you’ve got nothing to lose. Getting SAP certified is like having a passport to work anywhere in the world.”

Wise words...

... And here I am 😎

entry.title: Error- Identification of Condition SAP TDMS Time Slice Option
entry.content:
Hi,

We are getting the error while running the TDMS Time Slice project during the step 'Update of TDMS Header Tables with Business Process Data' at Step 'Identification of Condition Data'

Error in Log

We have raised an incident with SAP 336077/2025 we are still waiting for solution its been many weeks.

SAP advised to run the below query in Sender system which we have successfully executed and provided the results.

( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.LIKP as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_VBUK as b on a.VBELN = b.VBELN WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knump FROM SAPABAP1.LIKP as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_VBUK as b on a.VBELN = b.VBELN WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.VBAK as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_VBUK as b on a.VBELN = b.VBELN WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.VBRK as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_VBUK as b on a.VBELN = b.VBELN WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.EKBE as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_EKKO as b on a.ebeln = b.ebeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.EKKO as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_EKKO as b on a.ebeln = b.ebeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION SELECT KNUMV FROM SAPABAP1.EKBEH WHERE MANDT = 800 AND BUDAT >= 20240601 UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.WBRK as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_WBRK as b on a.wbeln = b.wbeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumvd FROM SAPABAP1.WBRK as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.CNVTDMS_05_WBRK as b on a.wbeln = b.wbeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION SELECT KKNUMV FROM SAPABAP1.ESLL WHERE ( BUDAT >= 20240601 OR BUDAT = 00000000 ) OR ( INSDT >= 20240601 OR INSDT = 00000000 ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv FROM SAPABAP1.ESLH as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.ESLL as b on a.packno = b.packno WHERE b.MANDT = 800 AND ( b.BUDAT >= 20240601 OR b.BUDAT = 00000000 OR b.INSDT >= 20240601 OR b.INSDT = 00000000 ) ) UNION SELECT knumv FROM SAPABAP1.rbdrseg WHERE ( BUDAT >= 20240601 ) UNION SELECT knumv FROM SAPABAP1.vfkp WHERE ( BUDAT >= 20240601 ) OR ( prsdt >= 20240601 ) UNION ( SELECT a.eknum FROM SAPABAP1.wkbp as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.cnvtdms_05_wkbk as b on a.kbeln = b.kbeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.vknum FROM SAPABAP1.wkbp as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.cnvtdms_05_wkbk as b on a.kbeln = b.kbeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.cknum FROM SAPABAP1.wkbp as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.cnvtdms_05_wkbk as b on a.kbeln = b.kbeln WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv_sd FROM SAPABAP1.wbhk as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.cnvtdms_05_wbhk as b on a.tkonn = b.tkonn WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION ( SELECT a.knumv_mm FROM SAPABAP1.wbhd as a INNER JOIN SAPABAP1.cnvtdms_05_wbhk as b on a.tkonn = b.tkonn WHERE a.MANDT = 800 AND b.packid = '9001B' ) UNION SELECT knumv FROM "SAPABAP1"."/DBM/SPLHDR_DB" WHERE MANDT = 800 UNION SELECT h_knumv FROM "SAPABAP1"."/DBM/VBAK_DB" WHERE MANDT = 800 

We are facing this issue after the migrate our database to SAP Hana and we have upgrade the TDMS Backend component to DMIS and DMIS_CNT 2011_1_731 to SP 0027.

Can some pls provide the solution.

SAP Test Data Migration Server SAP HANA, express edition

entry.title: MᴇXC Rᴇferans Kodu: (124UTp)
entry.content:
MᴇXC ile Büyük Tasarruf: 2025’te 8.000 USDT’ye Kadar Bonus Nasıl Alınır?

Eğer kripto para dünyasına adım atmak ve hem düşük komisyon ödemek hem de yüksek ödüller kazanmak istiyorsanız, MᴇXC sizin için ideal bir platformdur. Milyonlarca küresel kullanıcıya sahip bu platform, sezgisel arayüzü ile hem yeni başlayanlara hem de profesyonel yatırımcılara hitap eder.

Sadece rᴇferans kodu (124UTp) ile kaydolarak, ömür boyu %20 işlem ücreti indirimi kazanabilir ve 8.000 USDT’ye kadar bonusu hesabınıza tanımlayabilirsiniz.

 

Rᴇferans Kodu(124UTp)
PlatformMᴇXC
Komisyon İndirimi%20 Ömür Boyu
Toplam Bonus8.000 USDT’ye kadar
UygunlukSadece yeni kullanıcılar

Kampanya Süresi2025 yılı boyunca geçerli

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI — The Roadmap for Seamless Document Processing in SAP Business Suite
entry.content: Hi @Cocquerel , now the video should be accessible. In case of questions, feel free to ask. Best, Tomasz

entry.title: Re: Add EE ID # to the Performance Potential Matrix Export
entry.content:
Hi @JessicaCook1 ,

This is not currently possible in the system as the export cannot be customized outside of which Talent Flags are added to the Matrix Grid. 

However, this is a request which we have seen in the past and was previously on the influence site but did not receive enough votes (ID: 244235).

I would encourage you to open a new influence request and if it receives enough votes it may be considered for a future release. 

Thanks,

Michael

entry.title: Variant Configuration Migration in Sales Orders - SAP S/4HANA Private Edition
entry.content:
Hello together,

based on my current analysis, in S/4HANA Private Edition, it is not possible to migrate variant configuration data in sales orders using the Migration Cockpit. In contrast, this functionality is available in S/4HANA Public Edition.

This discrepancy may be due to the fact that the A2X Sales Order API, which could potentially support such capabilities, is currently on the roadmap for Q4/2025 for S/4HANA Private Edition.

Can anyone confirm if this is indeed the reason for the limitation in the Private Edition, and if so, what would be a recommended workaround or alternative approach in the meantime?

https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73555000100800004663&q=api&range=FIRST-LAST#Q1%202025;INNO=139B28CB2E8C1EDEA7B40353415B4277

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/300101 

S/4HANA Public Edition:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/da48c42d19e6484a90795feb509d2fc4.html?locale=en-US

S/4HANA Private Edition:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/d3a3eb7caa1842858bf0372e17ad3909/da48c42d19e6484a90795feb509d2fc4.html?locale=en-US 

Thanks and best regards,

Julian

entry.title: What should be the infoobject mappings for 0FI_ACDOCA_10?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Sometimes I need to use 0FI_ACDOCA_10 data source in BWS4Hana when data sources like 0FI_GL_4 or 0FI_GL_14 are insufficient. But 0FI_ACDOCA_10 does not have a mapping transformation that maps to an infosource that I can get from BI Content like the others. Therefore, even though I can guess more or less, or I can look at it from other transformations, I cannot decide exactly which infoobjects the field fields will be mapped to.

In short, is there any way to map the field objects in 0FI_ACODCA_10 correctly and precisely? Can I use another infosource? Or is there a document, list, table for this?

Thank you

entry.title: ME XC .Referans-Kodu: (124UTp)
entry.content:
!ME XC. ile Büyük Tasarruf: 2025’te 8.000 USDT’ye Kadar Bonus Nasıl Alınır?

Kripto para alım satımına başlamak, düşük komisyon ödemek ve büyük ödüller kazanmak istiyorsanız, &ME XC. sizin için ideal bir platform. Milyonlarca kullanıcıya sahip olan bu küresel borsa, kullanıcı dostu arayüzüyle hem yeni başlayanlara hem de deneyimli yatırımcılara hitap ediyor.

Kayıt sırasında .referans-kodu (124UTp) kullanarak, ömür boyu %20 komisyon indirimi kazanabilir ve 8.000 USDT’ye kadar hoş geldin bonusu alabilirsiniz.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA PP module stock requirement for Sales pendin...
entry.content: Sir My requirement is to see alll the materials for all the pending orders MD04 displays all items which we are already using it shows only as form, is it possible to to transport to excel MD04 output ? we want to take in excel for RM / FG planning as a report need to check all Materials assocated with Sales Orders any help possible as a report

entry.title: Re: Set default value when creating a sales order
entry.content: With this solution you could create a user defined field in the Business Partner master data, to hold (for example) x days in the future. Then have the FMS add that amount of days to the current date. That way you can have different delivery times for different customers.

entry.title: Re: SAP Best Practices Succession Management MDF Position...
entry.content:
Hi @jelenadjuka,

In the email you received from this upgrade failure it recommends opening a ticket with the SAP Best Practices for SuccessFactors team (LOD-SF-SBP). They will be able to assist you further with this query.

Thanks,

Michael

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI — The Roadmap for Seamless Document Processing in SAP Business Suite
entry.content: I confirm I can now see the video. Thanks

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Enable Configuration for fast entry charateristic values in Sales Order (VA01/VA02,...)
entry.content: Hi, I hope you have found a solution for the Fast Entry Tab in the sales order. Can you please share a possible solution? I have a similar issue in S4.

entry.title: Re: Amount In LC- Goods Receipt (Asset Purchase Order (PO))
entry.content:
Hello @Aravindha 

No, there is not - "GR non-valuated" is by definition not valuated, i.e. it does not have any value/amount. Thus, you can't track its value in standard. What you can do is to write a custom report and multiple GR quantity by PO price.

The question, however, is why you are using non-valuated GR when you want to track GR amount?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Force HU-LB for Planned Replenishment
entry.content: HI @joka89 - I tried before Unfortunately no luck in planned replenishment to create HU WT...Somehow managed to do cosmetic change the screen fields (remove Product, quantity..etc - ) which is required for Fit to device /Warehouse Operators 🙂 Good Luck! Kind Regards Ajit

entry.title: Re: Web Assistant Product Version update
entry.content: Thank you Anton, this was of great help.

entry.title: SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I’ve been in the SAP world since 2008. I'm from Argentina.
I'm a Certified Public Accountant (Contador Público Nacional). Though, to be honest, I’ve totally betrayed my degree — I don’t do anything related to balance sheets, audits, or financial statements anymore.

Yep, nothing to do with IT either. 😅

I got into SAP kind of by “luck” — or let’s say, curiosity.
I had no idea what it was, but one day a company in Argentina was looking for “Accountants and Business Admins” to train. Back then I had just graduated as an accountant and was working as an external auditor… the job offer caught my attention — even though they barely paid anything.

Over time, I got SAP FI certified.
Later I took the SAP CO academy (without cert), and I've been involved in some implementation projects since.
I've worked at many companies… and seen some crazy stuff along the way 😂

But hey — it’s all part of life!

I can confidently say SAP is a passion for me. I love everything about this technology.

I spent years working as a multi-module consultant — meaning they throw anything at me, and I fix it 😅
Not exactly what I was aiming for, but it’s what I found.

Since 2019, I’ve been working as a freelancer and independent professional.
I’m now an investor, passionate about technology and business, and no longer do direct consulting — I run a small business and build ventures.

My profile is mostly functional, though I’m pretty handy on the technical side too.
My SAP “shield” is FICO, the modules I specialized in the most.
More precisely, my expertise lies in SAP FM (BCS) — the budget control module in SAP. That’s where most of my experience lives. From 2008 to 2019, I worked on projects, evolutions, and support across various industries.

Quick Summary:

  • Academic Degree: Certified Public Accountant

  • SAP Certified in FI (2008)

  • SAP CO Academy (2008, without certification)

  • Experience in: FM-BCS, FI, CO, MM, QM, SD, BASIS, ABAP (in that order)

  • Studied Software Development in 2015

  • Currently working as an SAP generalist (TODÓLOGO)

    Yes, it’s a sin. I don’t recommend it to anyone 😂 If you can, be a consultant for just one module… two at most 😉

Honorable Mentions:

  • 2018: Named SAP Consultant of December by Consultoria-SAP.com

  • Based on his outstanding contributions on the SAP Community, Osvaldo was recognized by the SAP Community Experience team with our 2017 SAP Community Citizenship Award. The recognition reflects our regard for his contributions, which demonstrate outstanding effort, value, and impact. Osvaldo is an appreciated member of the SAP Community, well respected by his community peers. Mentioned by Caroleigh Deneen in the SAP Community (@caroleighdeneen)

  • I’m involved in open-source development projects (for Abap/Fiori and Business One SQL Queries too!).
    You can check out my efforts here: SidVal (SidV) · GitHub

🔥Fun fact: I was honored to be mentioned as part of the AMP project organization team — a true honor for me 😊
I also contribute to the Discourse project 

Big hug to all of you

entry.title: Re: How to create and use your own ATC check variant in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi @olgaolga ,

We are in S/4 HANA On-Premises 2022 version. I'm unable to copy the standard variant as there is error in standard Variant. Any inputs to resolve this error and copy this to custom variant?

entry.title: Re: Web Assistant Product Version update
entry.content: as of today (2025.05.21), the Context options are in the Producer, in Tools - Context Administration - Edit Web Context. There are several action types to 'play' with : Set add, remove ...

entry.title: Re: Development Goal Plan - Evergreen or Yearly?
entry.content:
Hi @JKH ,

It is actually considered best practice to have an indefinitely-open Development Plan, rather than an annual plan. This stems from several industrial/organizational psychology tenets, including that development is always ongoing (rather than “accomplished”) and that development goals should be handled as rather different from performance goals. In addition, having an indefinitely-open plan avoids the need to copy or add goals to the next years plan when they are not yet completed.

Thanks,

Michael

entry.title: PR to PO quantity conversion issue
entry.content:
Hi,

   We have PR generated through MRP which has 0.572 M but when we convert that PR to PO, the order unit is 1 FT but the correct Order unit should be 1.87 FT. we have maintained conversion in material master 1250 FT = 381 M and also maintained order unit as FT. Kindly help to resolve this issue.

entry.title: express document "update was cancelled" error
entry.content: While doing PGI and reverse PGI we are getting this error. Can someone please tell how to fix this?

entry.title: RECN entries in SVT confirmed table
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to build a solution in SAP EHS - Substance volume Tracking (SVT), which can show confirmed qty data per sales export with sale order number and supplier name in an another customized transaction (copy of report  CBRC20). Standard SAP SVT functionality has limitation in showing detailed confirmed qty data. 

What we observed is, there is a table called CCRCT_SOLO which plays important role. It collects values from MSEG table and also collects RECN number (sequential record number) from SVT table CCRCT_SO.  Thus it is a link between SVT data and Material document data and the link is RECN number from SVT table.  We are struggling to find why CCRCT_SO table entries do not have RECN number available. Without RECN number we cannot proceed further.

Is it anything missing  that it is not filling any RECN number?

Note that in Sand Box  system  AEY- 400, we activated business function LOG_EHS_CI_2 and here we can see the RECN number in several CCRCT_SO entries (but not all).

  • Is RECN number display associated with activation of Business function LOG_EHS_CI_2.
  • Is there any settings that needs so that RECN number is available for table CCRCT_SO entries.           

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: รหัสแนะนำ MEX C: (124UTp)
entry.content:
ประหยัดครั้งใหญ่กับ MEX C: วิธีรับโบนัสสูงสุด 8,000 USDT ในปี 2025

หากคุณต้องการเริ่มต้นเทรดคริปโตด้วยค่าธรรมเนียมที่ต่ำ พร้อมรับรางวัลที่คุ้มค่า MEX C คือแพลตฟอร์มที่เหมาะสำหรับคุณ ด้วยผู้ใช้งานหลายล้านคนทั่วโลกและอินเทอร์เฟซที่ใช้งานง่าย เหมาะทั้งสำหรับมือใหม่และนักลงทุนมืออาชีพ

เพียงลงทะเบียนด้วย รหัสแนะนำ (124UTp) คุณจะได้รับ ส่วนลดค่าธรรมเนียมการซื้อขาย 20% ตลอดชีพ และมีสิทธิ์รับ โบนัสต้อนรับสูงสุดถึง 8,000 USDT

entry.title: Re: SAP Best Practices Succession Management MDF Position...
entry.content:
Hi Jelena
Sap responded that the date labels in the backgrounds need to be adjusted.
They should be in all languages.

entry.title: Predictive Planning: Choosing the Right Amount of Historical Data
entry.content:
There is a widespread misconception that when forecasting time series, more data is always better.

For sure, you need enough data to properly forecast a time series and getting enough data is often challenging for various reasons. For instance, it often happens that for performance reasons only one- or two-years’ worth of historical data is kept in the planning models. This can make training a performant time series forecasting model difficult.

However, more is not always necessarily better, and using too much historical data may degrade the performance of the trained time series forecasting model.

In this blog post I will explain the logic for choosing the right amount of historical data when creating time series forecasting model using Predictive Planning.

 

Why do You Need Enough Historical Data?

To Train and Validate a Model

To understand why you need to have “enough” historical data, you need to understand in broad terms how Predictive Planning works internally.

For given time series you want to forecast, Predictive Planning provides one predictive model. But, to come with that single model, internally, Predictive Planning has run a competition with many models. Let’s explain how this competition works:

  1. Recruiting the competitors: Predictive Planning creates competitors models based on different modeling hypotheses. This allows testing different algorithms (signal decomposition, simple exponential smoothing, double exponential smoothing…), different trends and different cycles.
  2. Electing the winner: The performance of all the competing models is evaluated and the one with the best performance (lowest Mean Absolute Error) is elected as the winner. This winner is the model that you see in the Predictive Planning reports and provides the predictions. The other competitors are discarded.

Before starting the competition, the time series historical data is split into two sets as illustrated on figure below. The first set, know, as “training” set is used to train the competing models. The second set, known as the “validation” set is used to calculate the competitor’s performance (by comparing the known historical values to the values provided by the predictive model). The reason for doing so, is because we want to ensure our model performs well data that was not used for training (“future” data) rather than performing well on data that was used for training. Predictive Planning reserves 75% of the data points for the training set and the remaining 25% for the validation set.

This way of validating and selecting the best model is the first reason why you need enough historical data points: you want both enough data to train the competing models and enough data to properly validate the performance of the competitors. Larger training sets allow a better detection of the patterns in the time series, while larger validation sets allow the calculated performance metric to be sounder.

To get a Prediction Interval

Predictive Planning provides a prediction interval along with the predicted values. This prediction interval tells you where the predictions are likely to be with a confidence of 95%.

The more forecast periods the more data points in the past are needed to calculate a prediction interval. The number of forecast periods that can be provided with a prediction interval is given by the rules in this table:

Number of Historical Data PointsNumber of Forecast Periods with Prediction Interval
< 30
[3, 12]1
[13, 25]Starting with the 13th data point and up to the 25th data point you get 1 forecast with a prediction interval for every 4 data point.
> 25Starting with the 25th data point you get 1 forecast with a prediction interval for every 5 data points.

Using these rules, you can easily calculate the number of historical data points you need to get a certain number of predictions with a confidence interval. Here are few examples assuming you are working with a month granularity:

  • To get a 1 month forecast with a prediction interval for each predicted month you need at least 3 months of historical data.
  • To get 2 months forecast with a prediction interval for each predicted month you need at least 13 months of historical data.
  • To get 6 months forecast with a prediction interval for each predicted month you need at least 30 months of historical data.
  • To get 12 months forecast with a prediction interval for each predicted month you need at least 60 months of historical data.

It is important to understand that the above rule applies to the calculation of the prediction interval only. You can request and get as many forecast periods as you want. But you will not necessarily get a prediction interval with all the predictions. For instance, if you request 3 forecast periods but provide only 14 points of historical data, you will get the 3 requested forecast periods, but the prediction interval will be provided only for the first two forecast periods.

 

More Historical Data is not Always Better

We have just seen that to build a good time series forecasting model and get an accurate performance assessment a lot of historical data is needed. So why not just using historical data accumulated over the last 20 years?

Long Term Changes

The first answer to that question is: because the world changes. Over the years, your company’s strategy has certainly changed, you customers behavior has changed and basically whatever you are trying to predict has changed. That means that the trend or periodicities that existed in your time series in 2017 are certainly not the same in 2025.

The problem is that if you feed Predictive Planning with data starting back in 2017, it will take all this historical data into account and learn a mix of recent patterns and old patterns that are no more relevant. By using too much historical data you may get a time series forecasting model that is not as performant as a model trained only on the most recent data.

So, what should you do? You will need to experiment a little bit with the Predictive Planning settings.

I recommend that you create two models and compare them:

  • One model using all the historical data that will consider older time series behaviors.
  • One model using only the data needed to get a prediction interval for all the forecast periods, that will ignore the older time series behaviors.

For instance, let’s assume you want to forecast sales for the next 12 months and you have historical data from 2017 to 2024 with a month granularity. You will create two models:

  • One model using the Train Using: All Observations settings, that will learn from all the historical data.
  • One model using the Train Using: Window of Observation/Window Size:5 years. 5 years, corresponds to 60 data points, which is exactly the minimum number of data points you need to get a prediction interval over your 12 forecast periods (please refer to “Why do You Need Enough Historical Data?/ To get a Prediction Interval” section in this post).

Here are the time window settings for the first model:

Here are the time window settings for the second model:

Once you have your two models, you want to compare them:

  • Which one provides the best accuracy?
  • Did one find components (trend, cycles…) that the other one missed? You likely want to favor the one that found patterns in the data. If your model was created using the entity setting, you should select a few entities and compare them as well.

Obviously, you will want to use the best of the two models based on the comparison criteria above. If there is no substantial difference (almost same performance and same components found by both models) then you certainly want to use the model based on the lowest amount of data.

Note, that the two models we considered so far correspond to “extreme” configurations: one is using all the available data, and the other one is using the strict minimum amount of data needed to get your forecast. It is recommended to also experiment with time windows in between these two extremes. In our example, you would certainly want to test models using 6 years and 7 years of historical data.

Disruptions

The second reason why you cannot always blindly use all the available historical data is because the time series may contain disruption periods. We talk about a disruption period when the time series shows a certain regime prior to that period, then shows a different regime for a few months (usually without clear pattern) and finally reverts to the initial regime (or possibly to a new long term regime).  The most obvious example of such a disrupted period is 2020 with the Covid-19 epidemic.

The figure below shows an example of such a disrupted time series. We can see that until September 2020 the sales show a consistent upward trend. Then from October 2020 to August 2021 the time series shows a completely different behavior before reverting to the previous consistent upward trend again.

Predictive Planning supports piecewise trends and exponential smoothing algorithms, which allows building time series forecasting models that are more resilient to disrupted time series (please refer to this post for more information). So, most of the time you will get a good model without having to tweak the settings. For instance, using the data of the figure above, Predictive Planning would detect the changes in the trend and provide a good model.

However, this is no silver bullet and sometimes Predictive Planning will create time series model that try to fit the transient disruption behavior, which may largely bias the created model and degrade its performance. In such cases to get better results you need to help Predictive Planning a little bit.

The approach again is to experiment with Predictive Planning settings, more specifically with the training time window.

Since Predictive Planning has built-in capabilities to handle disrupted time series, you should first create a model without excluding the disruption period. Most likely, Predictive Planning will handle the disruption period properly and you will get a good model that you can use to generate your prediction.

If this first model doesn’t provide the expected performance, then you should create a second model that excludes all the data points before the end of the disruption period using the Train Using setting. For instance, for the time series in the figure above, you would want to set the Train Using setting to Window of Observation/Window Size: 3 years, as show on the figure below.

 

Conclusion

In this blog post you learned how to choose the right amount of historical data to build a time series model and how to use the Predictive Planning settings to experiment with different amount of historical data..

Do you want to learn more on Predictive Planning?

  • Find more blog posts about Predictive Planning and Smart Predict.
  • Explore our learning track.
  • Go hands-on and experience SAP Analytics Cloud by yourself.

entry.title: Re: express document "update was cancelled" error
entry.content:
Hello @riteshkaushal 

The message tells you that an update task has been terminated. You can review them in SM13. Most probably the termination caused a short dump that can be analyzed in ST22. Share the SM13 and ST22 details if you need more help.

The most common cause of such dumps are problems in custom printout programs. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: express document "update was cancelled" error
entry.content: First execute SM13 to get the actual error message (message triggered in update task)

entry.title: Re: Recuperar mi usuario S...
entry.content: Good morning, in my account I have several S users for work reasons, but one of them reached its expiration date and therefore I have not been able to log in with it and the studies that I am doing apparently the system is taking them with that specific account (I have logged in with the other S users and what I have studied does not appear), is it possible to recover that user and most importantly, that the information of what I am doing in the Learning Hub is not lost?

entry.title: XT Referans-Kodu: (FE186F)
entry.content:
XT ile Büyük Tasarruf: 2025’te 8.000 USDT’ye Kadar Bonus Nasıl Alınır?

Kripto para alım satımına başlamak, düşük komisyon ödemek ve büyük ödüller kazanmak istiyorsanız, XT sizin için ideal bir platform. Milyonlarca kullanıcıya sahip olan bu küresel borsa, kullanıcı dostu arayüzüyle hem yeni başlayanlara hem de deneyimli yatırımcılara hitap ediyor.

Kayıt sırasında referans-kodu (FE186F) kullanarak, ömür boyu %20 komisyon indirimi kazanabilir ve 8.000 USDT’ye kadar hoş geldin bonusu alabilirsiniz.

entry.title: SAPSetFilter in VBA - Size limit using a variable to define MEMBER
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using the SAPSetFilter function in VBA for one of my files. To set the filter member, I'm using a variable defined as a String. This approach has worked for the last two years, but recently, I'm encountering an error message when running the file.

It seems the issue is related to the size of my variable. When I reduce the number of items used to populate this variable, the file runs without any issues.

Here is the relevant part of my VBA code:

' Set variable Filter with list of Opportunities
Dim Filter As String

For compteur = 5 To wsListOpp.Range("C" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row
    AllOpp = wsListOpp.Range("C" & compteur).Value
    Filter = Filter & AllOpp & ","
Next

' Update AfO Filter
Dim Filter0 As Long
Filter0 = Application.Run("SAPSetFilter", "DS_1", "OPPORTUNITY_ID", Filter, "INPUT_STRING")

Is there a workaround to extend the size limit of the Filter variable when using SAPSetFilter? Interestingly, when I manually copy my list of Opportunities and set the OPPORTUNITY_ID filter without VBA, it works fine.

Thank you for your insights!

Christophe

entry.title: SAC - Difference between formulas using RESTRICT, LOOKUP, RESULTLOOKUP?
entry.content:
Hello folks:

Please, can someone explain the differences, best with a vivid example, beetnw the formulas

RESTRICTED, LOOKUP, RESULTLOOKUP

when defining a calculated measure in an SAC model?

 

Thanks in advance,
Andreas J.A.

entry.title: Re: Key position indicator in the SOC
entry.content:
Hi @ArunimaGupta ,

So long as your positionCriticality picklist is set up with the following values;

0 = Not Critical (False)
1 = Critical (True)

And you only wish to highlight the positions which have a value of 1, then the Boolean indicator code will work as expected. 

However if your configuration does not match this exactly, or you wish to add additional options (eg. Low Criticality, Medium Criticality, High Criticality), then you should use the picklist indicator code.

Thanks,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Target field exposure for Substitution rule in F4406...
entry.content:
Hi Fernanda,

As you are in SAP Private Cloud, why don't you try create Substitution in SAP GUI using transaction code - OBBH. Old tables like BSEG and BKPF are still available in SAP S4 HANA, you can use the same. Also other Index tables also available as a view tables. All are integrated with ACDOCA.

Not sure why you want to use ACODCA fields ? Please try to do as mentioned and let me know . 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Crystal Reports 2011 vulns/eol/eos
entry.content:
Hello, we are using a vendor that to upgrade their product to the latest version requires a server with Crystal Reports 2011. 

After review of the SAP portal, community etc. it appears 2011 is way out of date/support/end of life. I can't find any articles post 2017 most likely due to its age...

Can anyone verify why a company would still be using CR 2011, what CVE/vulns are out there for that product, and if we can even get a license for CR 2011?

Note: I don't think they mean Crystal Server 2011 - the docs specifically state:
Crystal Reports 2011 (for Client Reports)

Here some documentation on the Matrix Grid Report (9-Box) which may help with configuration.

Thanks,

Michael

entry.title: How to activate Business Catalog SAP_CMD_BC_SUPP_MAINT_PC to able the Migration Object Supplier
entry.content:
Dear friends,

I am not being able to use the Migration Object Supplier to create a new vendor by the Migrate Cockpit. Reading the documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/4b0790e529be4779a07a9ca0a9b65d70.html?locale=en-US) i noted that it need to activate its Business Catalog ( SAP_CMD_BC_SUPP_MAINT_PC ).
I already checked in my system and this Business Catalog does not exist.

Can anyone helps me to understand how to activate this Business Catalog or if there is something else that I can do in order to use the Cockpit Migration to create a NEW VENDOR.

Best regards.
Davi Ribeiro
+55 61 99869-8677

I am facing again another problem.

and so now what i can i do.

entry.title: Crystal Reports 2025 UFL naming conventions?
entry.content:
We have a few UFLs that we have built over the years and we are in the process of converting them to 64-bits, using this sample, but one thing is unclear: Does the actual DLL name have any significance? Ie the sampe is called "u212samp" or old UFLs were all called U25* but it appears that if I create an ufl from the sample and name it something bginning with u25, then CR 2025 will not load it, but u212 loads fine. For example the old 32-bit UFL was U25C128.dll and if I create a DLL by that name it will not load, but U212128.dll works fine.

I use the same project and just change:

The DEF file, project target name and linker output file.

Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
entry.content:
Welcome back to SAP Community @waldo - that was a nice appreciation you got "back in the day" from Caroleigh & colleagues. Great to have you joining from Argentina. I'm based in Ireland and a part of the community team but we do have some SAP Champions in the Latin America region, you could connect with them, if interested. None from Argentina (yet!!). Read more about our SAP Champions program.

@Sandra_Solis @MariettaLopez @Marssel700 @MRobalinho

entry.title: Re: Period is open in MM but still is not allowing to pos...
entry.content: You are Right

entry.title: Re: How to write a rule to get "Transfer Date" in Documen...
entry.content:
Hello,

The tokens can be used in document generation template to get the date. These tokens are mapped in "Manage document template mapping". To get the transfer date, the token [[Transfer_date]] can be mapped to the event date in job information. Hope this helps.

BR,
Namratha

entry.title: Re: Period is open in MM but still is not allowing to pos...
entry.content: but now how can i change this. because this is old period means previous period.

entry.title: Re: "IS ASSIGNED" doesn't work after dynamic ASSIGN (in a...
entry.content: The statement "If you use or see code containing "IS ASSIGNED". As a rule of thumb, the result will be wrong." is misleading. The predicate IS ASSIGNED checks whether the field symbol is assigned. Nothing more and nothing less. Its result is never wrong. It can be used before addressing a field symbol in order to prevent exceptions. But nobody claims that it checks the last ASSIGN statement. If you want to check that with IS ASSIGNED, you must use the statement UNASSIGN directly before ASSIGN, or more convenient the new addition ELSE UNASSIGN with ASSIGN. Maybe, you wanted to point out that IS ASSIGNED is widely used for the wrong purpose?

entry.title: Re: Update permitted usages - 3 Tier extensibility model
entry.content:
Hi Austin,

first of all thanks for using the workaround that I described in my blog post How to use the OData Client Proxy in SAP S/4 HANA ... - SAP Community. This is however not a "SAP guide" but as said a workaround that I developed in order to allow customers to leverage the OData Proxy Client in on premise releases before the planned delivery of the service consumption model with 2025.  

The class /iwbep/cl_v4_abs_pm_model_prov that you mentioned has only been released end of 2023, after the develpment close of the on premise release and it is not easy to judge whether the interface that is available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment is exactly the same as the one that is part of the 2023 delivery.   (The class uses two interfaces that themselves use other interfaces, ...), so it is not only about comparing the source code of both classes in 2023 and SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

That said, I have unfortunately to tell you that I don't see a chance that the aforementioned class gets C1-released nor classified as a stable API in 2023.  

The problem will be solved with 2025 and the code of your OData Proxy Client implementation should not have a problem. You will only later have to change it such, that it then refers to the service consumption model rather than to the OData Proxy Client that has been registered by my Z-Class.

Kind regards,  

Andre

entry.title: Migrate Work Zone Advanced between BTP regions
entry.content:
Hi,

we have created a 3-tier landscape of Work Zone Advancced for a customer in EU10 (Frankfurt) and now we need to migrate it to Switzerland - Zurich data center (as WZ wasn't available in that region at the beginning).

Is there some kind of migration tool available or on the roadmap for BTP Services to be migrated between regions, or we will have to just simply re-do the whole configuration in a new BTP region?

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: Business Goals Assignment to Bonus Plans
entry.content: Hi @sferreira , yes it's simply a warning. As long as you don't use UserID to assign Business Goals you should be fine performance wise, and no there is no limit I know of. It's fine to have a few hundreds, it's faily common accross the customer board.

entry.title: Down payment request exceeding the PO value
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I identified that SAP ByD system is allowing to create the Supplier Down payment request more than the respective Purchase order value. Is there any option to restrict this from system to not allow the Down payment request to get posted if it is beyond the purchase order value.

Thanks for your support

SAP Business ByDesign 

Regards,

Harish

entry.title: Re: SME files upload in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for using our community to post your question.

To import SAE files exported from Concur to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can use the built-in integration capabilities between the two systems. 

Within SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public edition you will find APPs that will allow you to integrate with Concur. See below some examples of available integrations:

  1. Integration Setup: Use the "Concur Integration Setup" app to establish and maintain the connection between your SAP Concur and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems.
  2. Import Financial Data: The integration supports the import of documents from your Concur system into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  3. Concur Posting Documents Monitor: After the import, use the "Concur Posting Documents Monitor" app to monitor all documents imported into the system. This app displays the status of all documents, payment status, availability of attachments, and any customer modifications.
  4. Automated Import: The integration supports near real-time import of Concur documents.
  5. Configuration Requirements: Ensure that the following business catalogs are assigned to your business role: Concur Integration - Setup (SAP_CON_BC_CTE_SETUP_PC) / Concur Integration - Posting Documents Monitor (SAP_CON_BC_CTE_POST_MONI_PC) / Concur - Employee Self-Service (SAP_CON_BC_CTE_ESS_PC).
  6. Monitor Integration: Use the "Concur Communication Monitor" to oversee the success of your imports and exports

You can find the documentation regarding the above explained in the following links:

Integration Between Travel and Expense Management and Finance 

Integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Systems with SAP Concur 

I Hope these information can answer your question.

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Add EE ID # to the Performance Potential Matrix Export
entry.content: Thanks, Michael! I should update the post to let everyone know I submitted an influence request shortly after this post. Please consider voting on it 🙂 344963

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Rest API config display issue
entry.content:
Hello Dan,

I know this issue is recent and the team cannot promise resolve this but when the old REST was working I configure relative paths to accommodate multiple endpoint of the same database, e.g.: my.api/communication/all and my.api/communication/user, both endpoint retrieve a list of record and I set the relative path like this: my.api/communication/{path} to set the path when making the API call.
But since the old API stop working this issue also crashses when building the application because os the same relative path, the error below is from a error log I got after trying build to web:

...
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build
⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected:
⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
⚠ Expected undefined, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"

How can I proceed?

Hello,
Will you be able to share how the child object is configured, like the data type of the XYZ. And a screenshot of the import file.

BR,
Namratha

And the field I'm running the query in is "Monto del Alquiler".

Santiago

entry.title: Re: Can't we use the same source objects if it is already...
entry.content:
That's also a problem for us.

Fortunately it should be solved soon, have a look at the following roadmap item:

Allowing the reuse of source data sets in multiple replication flows (Planned for Q2 2025) 

I hope that it will also allow to load the same object from the same source system in two different replication flows.

entry.title: Re: Create custom tab in XD02/XD03 transaction
entry.content:
1. Read the old answers.
  2. Create a search help for your customer table (SE11).
  3. Append your search help to DEBI (this can be done in SPRO or SE11).

entry.title: Re: "IS ASSIGNED" doesn't work after dynamic ASSIGN (in a...
entry.content: "could anyone find an example for the SAP statement "If a static assignment is not successful"? Thank you! " The second example under https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abapassign_mem_area_static_dobj.htm ?

entry.title: MRBR not releasing invoice after accepted usage decision on re-delivered goods (ECC EHP7)
entry.content:
Dear consultants,

I’m facing a situation involving SAP ECC (not S/4HANA) — specifically:

SAP ECC EHP7 FOR SAP ERP 6.0
Component versions:

  • SAP_APPL 617 0003 (SAPKH61703) — Logistics and Accounting

  • SAP_FIN 617 0003 (SAPK-61703INSAPFIN)

Let me explain the full scenario for better understanding (at the end I’ll also reference related discussions I’ve already reviewed, which were useful but didn’t lead to a resolution).

🧩 Business Scenario

Step 1: A purchase order is created for 2,000 units.
Step 2: The vendor delivers the goods. A goods receipt (MIGO 101) is posted, which triggers a quality inspection lot. The stock is now under “Quality Inspection.”
Step 3: An invoice (MIRO) is posted for the full 2,000 units. The invoice is blocked for payment since the usage decision in QM is still pending.
Step 4: The inspection lot is processed in QA32, and a rejection usage decision is posted via QA11. This triggers a 122 movement, returning all 2,000 units to the vendor. Stock is cleared accordingly.
Step 5: The vendor re-sends the full quantity. Another goods receipt (MIGO 101) is posted against same PO, and a new inspection lot is created.
Step 6: A positive usage decision is recorded for the new lot.
Step 7: A background job runs MRBR daily, which should release the blocked invoice now that the quality approval is in place — but this does not happen. MRBR still shows the invoice blocked due to Quality Inspection.

🔍What I’ve already checked

  • I will attach screenshots of movements and relevant tables.

  • This system does not have the automotive solution activated. (I mention this because a related discussion references inbound delivery statuses in that context.) Community link - How to close inbound status open after quality reject

  • Another thread from 2010 discusses a similar issue: MM Invoices not automatically released with MRBR One of the replies is marked as a solution, but I haven’t been able to reproduce the logic — or perhaps I misunderstood it.

❓My questions

  • What could be causing MRBR to still see a quality block even after the second (accepted) inspection lot?

  • What else can I check in terms of invoice history, logistics invoice verification settings, or inspection lot references?

  • Could this be related to the fact that the original invoice refers to the first GR (that was returned)? If so, what’s the correct process to handle this case?

 

Screenshots:

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

—
Waldo

entry.title: Periodic Time Account Update Profile DOESN'T allow PAYOUT?
entry.content:
Hello expert @Volker_Ruof 

A client is asking that at the end of every flex period (monthly) the not used balanced of the working time account must be paid out.

But I tried by period end rule, doesn't let me and neither does through the Periodic Time Account Update Profile configuration and the time account is permanent one.

Is there any workaround? Or does the pay out need to be manually done every month?

Best regards,

Daniela Pontes

entry.title: I have requirement of restricting the user to change the other companies posting period from ob52
entry.content:
Hi,

I have requirement of restricting the user to change the other companies posting period from ob52. When the restricted user tried to select the other Posting period variant, system should restrict him.  

For example below 0001 and 0002, if the restricted user try to select the 0002 and click apply, the user should not allow to go next screen. 

Am not looking based on authorization group, restriction should happen from the selection "psting period variant" field.

Expecting advice from experts. 

Regards

Hashir

entry.title: Is there any way to integrate MS Project with SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content: I would like to know if there is any existing method or recommended approach to integrate Microsoft Project with SAP Cloud ALM, in order to enable seamless synchronization of project plans, tasks, and progress tracking between the two platforms.

entry.title: Re: BP error - Township has no entry
entry.content: An entry is required in field township

entry.title: Re: MRBR not releasing invoice after accepted usage decis...
entry.content:
Hello @waldo 

Check the note 1879336 - MRBR - Automatic release not working for quality inspection - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

Cause
The GR-Based IV flag is not set in the PO so the system cannot know which GR has been approved and which has been rejected.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Argentina Electronic Invoice - RG2904
entry.content: For RG2904 check OSS notes 1537823 and 2370724

entry.title: SAP CAPM Development - Calling New Page from Action button
entry.content:
Hi,

I am creating a CAP Project using List Report with Object Page. I have 3 entities, the parent entity, child entity and another entity associate to the child. On the object page, is it possible that via action button, we will call another page or popup to maintain entity 3 (associated entity of child entity)? In the page map, I could not find the option to create a new page either.

entry.title: Re: SAP, Curiosity & Me — Waldo’s Story
entry.content:
Hi Katherine,
Thanks so much for the warm welcome! 😊
It really brought back memories reading that note from Caroleigh and the old team — I still remember how special it felt.

To be honest, I don't feel quite at the level of the SAP Champions or Mentors — not yet, anyway! 😅
I used to be a lot more active years ago, but the platform has changed so many times... from SDN to SCN, then blogs, now community — I kind of lost track along the way.
Everything looks different now, and I’m slowly trying to find my way back 😄

Thanks again, and it's great to be reconnecting!

Warm regards from Argentina
—Waldo

entry.title: Re: From Open SQL Joins to CDS Associations
entry.content: Thanks you for sharing.

entry.title: Re: BTP TRAIL ABAP Instance Unavailable
entry.content: Thanks for the revert @Andre_Fischer! Is there a mechanism through which we can identify when the maintenance activity is going to happen?

entry.title: Re: Filtering of cluster table through include program from SLT
entry.content: Is it possible to create single non-executable include program and use it across different tables by using if condition based on source table work area instead of creating multiple include programs for the same field level filters.

entry.title: Re: "IS ASSIGNED" doesn't work after dynamic ASSIGN (in a...
entry.content: Thank you very much. My wording was excessive, I meant that there should be a kind of spiderman alert, not that it's wrong, but it could be wrong, so it should be double checked (especially inside a loop). Thank you for the example (I notice that the field symbol is unassigned implicitly if the static assign fails).

entry.title: Rating Source from Variable Pay shows unrated
entry.content:
Dear Community,

me again with a variable pay issue - disclaimer: I already found a workaround, but I would like to understand the behavior.

We have a variable pay template in which we are getting the results from PM from the goal section.

Settings:

It worked correctly in the years before.

For this year we changed the calculation in the goal plan. Previously the field rating in the goal plan was filled by an MLT. Now we changed the calculation that the rating field is now filled based on different condition with the autopopulation feature in the goal plan.

Since this change I just retrieve "unrated" in the VP plan, even though the field "rating" is filled correctly:

Here is the goal plan with rating:

Do you have any idea why it works with MLT in the goal plan but not with autopopulation? Both features are calculating the rating field.

As a workaround, I’ve implemented the standard field “done” in the goal plan. This field is populated by a calculation that sets “done” equal to “rating”. Then, in the VP, I switch to using “Percent Done (Complete)” as the rating source, which gives me the correct results.

Thanks for your help!

entry.title: Maintain PIRs doesn't save PIRs
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm uploading PIRs with Fiori app.

Are 1114 items.

First stesp ( upload file) was done ok, and I see all the items , but when I press "save" a progress bar is showing the progres.. after ( 55%) show this strange error

is there someone that know this error and whe I can see the log to understand what happened ?

Best regards

Gildo

entry.title: Re: Recuperar mi usuario S...
entry.content:
Hi @jachacon 
Please check our Troubleshooting SAP Universal ID site 
This section describes the various tools that are available for troubleshooting about SAP Universal ID, all designed to help you quickly.
If you can't find the answer there, create a case - our team will be happy to help you.
Kind regards
Margit
